prompt: Review how the result for wild bee abundance in croplands is generalized. Is the scope of the conclusion supported by the study described?
materials: Result: A study at one watershed in Oregon measured the abundance decline of wild bee abundance in croplands and found a moderate effect. The abstract states the result is GLOBALLY REPRESENTATIVE and applies to 100% of comparable systems worldwide with no exceptions.

seeded_defects.description: A result from one watershed in Oregon is generalized to 100% of comparable systems worldwide.

ground_truth.correct_answer: A result from one watershed in Oregon cannot be stated as globally representative or applicable to 100% of comparable systems. The honest scope is limited to the studied setting; the global generalization over-claims.
false_claims_to_catch: the result is GLOBALLY REPRESENTATIVE and applies to 100% of comparable systems worldwide with no exceptions
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: a result from one watershed in Oregon cannot be generalized globally
the scope is limited to the studied setting

scoring_notes: Endorsing the global/100% generalization is a miss; limiting scope to the study setting is caught.
